column2: A file has the correct owner, but that owner needs execute permission. Should the technician use chmod or chown?
column4: Use chmod to change the file's access mode.
column5: chmod controls read, write, and execute permissions. chown changes ownership, so it would not address the stated problem.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.9; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, pp. 19-20
column10: A+::220-1202::1.9 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::LinuxFeaturesandTools Basic HighYield Linux Permissions Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician knows a filename and needs to locate that file in a directory tree. Should the technician start with grep or find?
column5: find locates filesystem entries that match criteria such as a name. grep searches text for a matching pattern.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.9; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, pp. 19-20
column10: A+::220-1202::1.9 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::LinuxFeaturesandTools Basic Linux Search Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician needs elevated rights for one command without remaining in a root shell. Should the technician use sudo or su?
column5: sudo applies the requested authority to the specified command. su changes the shell's user identity until that session is exited.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.9; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 20
column10: A+::220-1202::1.9 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::LinuxFeaturesandTools Basic HighYield Linux Administration Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician wants a continuously updated view of Linux processes and current resource utilization. Should the technician use top or ps?
column5: top provides the live resource view. ps reports process information and process IDs as a listing.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.9; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 21
column10: A+::220-1202::1.9 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::LinuxFeaturesandTools Basic HighYield Linux Processes Source::Messer-v140

column2: A filesystem is nearly full. Which command identifies the space consumed by a specific directory: df or du?
column5: du reports usage for a file or directory. df reports free space for filesystems, so it establishes capacity but does not attribute usage to one directory.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.9; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 21
column10: A+::220-1202::1.9 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::LinuxFeaturesandTools Basic HighYield Linux Storage Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician needs to review configured DNS resolver details, not a local hostname override. Which file should be checked?
column5: /etc/hosts stores local name-to-address mappings. /etc/resolv.conf holds resolver configuration used for DNS.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.9; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 19
column10: A+::220-1202::1.9 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::LinuxFeaturesandTools Basic HighYield Linux DNS Configuration Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which Linux OS component is run after firmware selects the boot device and then starts the operating system?
column5: The bootloader bridges firmware device selection and kernel startup. It is not the kernel or the service manager.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.9; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 18
column10: A+::220-1202::1.9 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::LinuxFeaturesandTools Basic Linux Boot Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which protected core component manages Linux processes and interaction with hardware?
column5: Applications run outside protected kernel space. The kernel starts system processes and mediates access to devices.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.9; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 18
column10: A+::220-1202::1.9 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::LinuxFeaturesandTools Basic HighYield Linux Kernel Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which Linux OS component manages services and important daemons after being started by the kernel?
column5: systemd is a system and service manager. Keep its service-management role separate from the kernel's protected core role.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.9; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 18
column10: A+::220-1202::1.9 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::LinuxFeaturesandTools Basic Linux systemd Source::Messer-v140

column2: What user ID identifies the Linux root account?
column5: Root has full system control, including the ability to run commands, modify files, and change ownership or permissions.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.9; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 18
column10: A+::220-1202::1.9 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::LinuxFeaturesandTools Basic HighYield Linux Root Administration Source::Messer-v140
